street.thesis: CWST owns irreplaceable Northeast landfill franchises with monopoly-like pricing power; the adj-FCF ramp to $200-210M in FY2026 (from $85M in FY2025) marks the inflection from M&A assembly to steady-state cash generation. Bull thesis: franchise moat + integration synergies re-rate EV/EBITDA toward RSG/WCN levels (17-21x) as margin expands from 23% toward 28%+.

history_context.note: CWST 5-year median EV/EBITDA ~14.8x; peaked at 19-20x in 2021-2022 on M&A optionality premium; compressed to 13-14x in 2025-2026 on rate sensitivity and acquisition dilution

duality_decomposition.note: single-stage justified forward P/E = (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g); shows the ROIC/payout lever

peers.name: WM
peers.note: Waste Management, NTM EV/EBITDA; 29% EBITDA margin; large-cap mature

peers.name: RSG
peers.note: Republic Services, NTM EV/EBITDA; 32% EBITDA margin; large-cap mature

peers.name: WCN
peers.note: Waste Connections, NTM EV/EBITDA; 33% EBITDA margin; growth premium

peers.name: GFL
peers.note: GFL Environmental, NTM EV/EBITDA; ~22% EBITDA margin; integration discount

reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 14.0x FY2026E Adj. EBITDA ($B), the current price implies a ~9.6% FCF CAGR for 10y (then 2% terminal) at r=8.0%.
reconstruction.what_street_implies: The street mean target implies ~12.4% for 10y.
reconstruction.history_anchor: CWST 5-year median EV/EBITDA ~14.8x; peaked at 19-20x in 2021-2022 on M&A optionality premium; compressed to 13-14x in 2025-2026 on rate sensitivity and acquisition dilution
reconstruction.contested_lever: EBITDA margin re-rate: whether CWST closes the 7-10pp gap to large-cap peers (WM/RSG 28-32%) through M&A integration synergies ($15M G&A target) and Highland/Hakes permit-enabled internalization, or whether the perpetual acquisition pace permanently suppresses blended margins below 26% and caps the multiple re-rating the bull thesis requires
